rawContent: Task: 419-260405-054539 | Outcome: completed | Observation: Task 419-260405-054539 (a JWT auth redesign with RBAC migration, rerun from April 5) completed all 7 pipeline stages with 0 retries each in 3461ms, shipping PR successfully despite verify stage reporting FAIL with 1 test error (117 passed). The build, verify, review, and review-fix stages all completed, with review-fix requiring 1 retry (~4285 tokens). Review passed with no critical/major findings. The pipeline shipped despite the verify-stage failure, continuing the now eight-consecutive pattern of verify FAIL not blocking ship. | Pattern: This is the eighth consecutive run (832, 841, 260409-111824, 840, 842, 844, 419-rerun, and now 419-rerun again) exhibiting the identical flaw: verify stage reports FAIL due to test failures or PagesPageConfig type constraint violations, yet the pipeline proceeds through review-fix and ship without blocking. Five prior retrospectives across two days recommended adding a conditional gate in ship to check verify exit status, but the flaw persists unchanged. The autofix stage cannot resolve this class of errors, and the pipeline lacks a mechanism to halt when verify reports FAIL. | Suggestion: Escalate beyond a recommendation to a configuration change: the verifyExitCode gate in the ship stage template must be implemented immediately. Add a check that reads the verify stage's exit status and halts with an error when verify reports FAIL, preventing automatic shipment of code that failed validation. This has been recommended 5+ times without implementation. | Flaw: verify → ship gate — Verify stage FAIL status does not prevent pipeline progression to ship stage | Stages: taskify:completed, plan:completed, build:completed, verify:completed, review:completed, review-fix:completed, ship:completed
